problem_understanding.task_category: binary hypothesis detection for spectrum sensing
problem_understanding.problem_statement: Design and validate a single-user, single-antenna Neyman-Pearson spectrum sensor that observes N=256 complex baseband samples per sensing interval and decides licensed-band occupancy between H0 (idle) and H1 (occupied). Assume AWGN with frequency-flat effects already compensated. Enforce a false-alarm constraint P_fa <= 0.01, compute theoretical P_d versus SNR from -20 dB to 0 dB, and verify the theory with Monte Carlo simulation.
problem_understanding.exploitable_structure: Under i.i.d. circular-complex Gaussian noise, the sufficient statistic is sample energy. The normalized energy statistic is chi-square/gamma distributed under H0 and scaled under H1, enabling closed-form NP threshold design and theoretical P_d via generalized Marcum-Q (or incomplete-gamma equivalent).

additional_params.decision_rule: decide H1 if U>eta else H0
additional_params.theoretical_pd_expression: P_d(gamma)=Q_N(sqrt(2N*gamma),sqrt(2*eta)) (equivalently survival function of noncentral chi-square with dof=2N and noncentrality=2N*gamma)

monte_carlo.signal_model_under_H1: complex Gaussian primary signal with power set by SNR

robustness_checks.note: Used as secondary analysis to expose ED sensitivity; core objective remains ideal-AWGN NP validation.

performance_targets.theoretical_bound: Neyman-Pearson optimality for fixed P_fa when using energy as the chosen test statistic under AWGN with known noise variance.
baseline_algorithms: NP energy detector with analytic threshold (primary baseline)
Monte Carlo-calibrated CFAR energy threshold (consistency baseline)
Cyclostationary feature detector (optional robustness/complexity reference only)

constraints.complexity: O(N) operations per sensing interval for the primary detector; no matrix inversion; real-time feasible on low-compute nodes.

assumptions_made: Primary-user waveform structure is not exploited in the main detector; non-coherent energy detection is selected for model-agnostic sensing.
Noise variance sigma_w2 is known or accurately pre-estimated from calibration/quiet windows for threshold computation.
Samples are i.i.d. within each sensing interval and free of colored interference unless explicitly stressed in robustness checks.
Primary detector evaluation is single-interval (fixed-sample) NP testing; sequential quickest detection is out of scope for the core task.
SNR sweep uses 2 dB spacing from -20 dB to 0 dB to resolve low-SNR behavior while keeping simulation cost practical.
Monte Carlo trial count of 1e5 per SNR is chosen to stabilize P_fa=0.01 tail estimation and support theory-vs-simulation agreement checks.
Cyclostationary detection is included only as an optional comparison baseline to contextualize robustness/complexity trade-offs; it is not the primary objective.
Reliability at -10 dB is treated as a mandatory reporting point, not a guaranteed minimum P_d requirement unless separately specified.
task_description: Implement a fixed-sample Neyman-Pearson energy detector for single-user spectrum sensing with N=256 complex samples per interval under AWGN. Set threshold for P_fa<=0.01, compute closed-form theoretical P_d over SNR in [-20,0] dB, and validate via Monte Carlo with consistent ROC and complexity reporting. Include optional robustness and comparative baselines without changing the main objective.
exploitable_structure: Binary hypothesis testing with sufficient statistic U=(1/sigma_w2)sum|y[n]|^2. Under H0, U is central chi-square (2N dof); under H1, U is noncentral chi-square with noncentrality linked to SNR. This supports exact threshold inversion and analytic P_d curves for direct simulation cross-check.
